title: Famous Battles
description: Major battles from American and world history

cards.front: Battle of Lexington & Concord (1775)
cards.back: First battles of the American Revolution; "shot heard round the world"

cards.front: What were the first battles of the American Revolution?
cards.back: Battle of Lexington & Concord (1775)

cards.front: Battle of Bunker Hill (1775)
cards.back: Early Revolutionary War battle near Boston; British won but suffered heavy casualties, boosting American morale

cards.front: Which Revolutionary War battle near Boston gave the Americans confidence despite being a British victory?
cards.back: Battle of Bunker Hill (1775)

cards.front: Battle of Trenton (1776)
cards.back: Washington crossed the Delaware River on Christmas night to surprise Hessian troops; key morale boost for the Continental Army

cards.front: In which battle did Washington cross the Delaware River on Christmas night?
cards.back: Battle of Trenton (1776)

cards.front: Battle of Saratoga (1777)
cards.back: Turning point of the American Revolution; American victory convinced France to ally with the United States

cards.front: What was the turning point of the American Revolution that brought France into the war?
cards.back: Battle of Saratoga (1777)

cards.front: Battle of Yorktown (1781)
cards.back: Last major battle of the American Revolution; British General Cornwallis surrendered to Washington, ending the war

cards.front: What was the last major battle of the American Revolution?
cards.back: Battle of Yorktown (1781)

cards.front: Battle of Fort Sumter (1861)
cards.back: First battle of the Civil War; Confederate forces fired on the Union fort in Charleston, South Carolina

cards.front: What was the first battle of the Civil War?
cards.back: Battle of Fort Sumter (1861)

cards.front: First Battle of Bull Run / Manassas (1861)
cards.back: First major land battle of the Civil War; Confederate victory in Virginia showed the war would not end quickly

cards.front: What was the first major land battle of the Civil War?
cards.back: First Battle of Bull Run / Manassas (1861)

cards.front: Battle of Shiloh (1862)
cards.back: Bloody Civil War battle in Tennessee; Union victory under Ulysses S. Grant; one of the war's costliest battles

cards.front: Which bloody 1862 Civil War battle in Tennessee was a Union victory under Grant?
cards.back: Battle of Shiloh

cards.front: Battle of Antietam (1862)
cards.back: Bloodiest single day in American history; Union strategic victory in Maryland that led Lincoln to issue the Emancipation Proclamation

cards.front: What was the bloodiest single day in American history?
cards.back: Battle of Antietam (September 17, 1862)

cards.front: Battle of Gettysburg (1863)
cards.back: Turning point of the Civil War; Union victory in Pennsylvania that ended Lee's invasion of the North

cards.front: What was the turning point battle of the Civil War?
cards.back: Battle of Gettysburg (1863)

cards.front: Battle of Vicksburg (1863)
cards.back: Union siege victory that gave the North control of the Mississippi River, splitting the Confederacy in two

cards.front: Which Civil War battle gave the Union control of the Mississippi River?
cards.back: Battle of Vicksburg (1863)

cards.front: Battle of Appomattox Court House (1865)
cards.back: Site where Confederate General Robert E. Lee surrendered to Union General Ulysses S. Grant, effectively ending the Civil War

cards.front: Where did Robert E. Lee surrender to Ulysses S. Grant to end the Civil War?
cards.back: Appomattox Court House, Virginia (1865)

cards.front: First Battle of the Marne (1914)
cards.back: World War I battle where French and British forces stopped the German advance on Paris, leading to trench warfare on the Western Front

cards.front: Which World War I battle stopped the German advance on Paris and led to trench warfare?
cards.back: First Battle of the Marne (1914)

cards.front: Battle of Gallipoli (1915-1916)
cards.back: Failed Allied campaign to capture the Ottoman capital of Constantinople; major defeat for Britain, France, and ANZAC forces

cards.front: Which World War I campaign was a failed Allied attempt to capture Constantinople from the Ottoman Empire?
cards.back: Battle of Gallipoli (1915-1916)

cards.front: Battle of Verdun (1916)
cards.back: Longest single battle of World War I; ten-month German offensive against France with massive casualties on both sides

cards.front: What was the longest single battle of World War I?
cards.back: Battle of Verdun (1916)

cards.front: Battle of the Somme (1916)
cards.back: One of the bloodiest battles in history; British and French offensive against Germany on the Western Front in World War I

cards.front: Which World War I battle on the Western Front was one of the bloodiest in history, with over one million casualties?
cards.back: Battle of the Somme (1916)

cards.front: Attack on Pearl Harbor (1941)
cards.back: Surprise Japanese attack on the U.S. naval base in Hawaii on December 7; brought the United States into World War II

cards.front: What event on December 7, 1941, brought the United States into World War II?
cards.back: Attack on Pearl Harbor

cards.front: Battle of Midway (1942)
cards.back: Turning point of the Pacific Theater in World War II; U.S. Navy destroyed four Japanese aircraft carriers

cards.front: What was the turning point battle of the Pacific Theater in World War II?
cards.back: Battle of Midway (1942)

cards.front: Battle of El Alamein (1942)
cards.back: British victory in North Africa that stopped the German advance toward the Suez Canal; turning point of the North African campaign

cards.front: Which World War II battle in North Africa stopped Rommel's advance toward the Suez Canal?
cards.back: Battle of El Alamein (1942)

cards.front: Battle of Stalingrad (1942-1943)
cards.back: Turning point of the Eastern Front in World War II; Soviet victory over Germany in one of the deadliest battles in history

cards.front: What was the turning point battle of the Eastern Front in World War II?
cards.back: Battle of Stalingrad (1942-1943)

cards.front: D-Day / Battle of Normandy (June 6, 1944)
cards.back: Allied invasion of Nazi-occupied France; largest seaborne invasion in history that began the liberation of Western Europe

cards.front: What was the largest seaborne invasion in history, launching the Allied liberation of Western Europe?
cards.back: D-Day / Battle of Normandy (June 6, 1944)

cards.front: Battle of the Bulge (1944-1945)
cards.back: Last major German offensive on the Western Front in World War II; Allied victory in the Ardennes forest region

cards.front: What was the last major German offensive of World War II?
cards.back: Battle of the Bulge (1944-1945)

cards.front: Battle of Iwo Jima (1945)
cards.back: Fierce World War II battle where U.S. Marines captured the island from Japan; famous flag-raising photograph on Mount Suribachi

cards.front: On which Pacific island did U.S. Marines raise the famous flag on Mount Suribachi?
cards.back: Iwo Jima (1945)

cards.front: Battle of Thermopylae (480 BC)
cards.back: Ancient battle where 300 Spartans and Greek allies held off a massive Persian army under Xerxes at a narrow pass

cards.front: At which ancient battle did 300 Spartans make a famous last stand against the Persian army?
cards.back: Battle of Thermopylae (480 BC)

cards.front: Battle of Marathon (490 BC)
cards.back: Ancient Greek victory over Persia; inspired the legend of Pheidippides running to Athens to announce the victory

cards.front: Which ancient battle inspired the modern marathon race?
cards.back: Battle of Marathon (490 BC)

cards.front: Battle of Hastings (1066)
cards.back: Norman victory over Anglo-Saxons in England; William the Conqueror defeated King Harold II, beginning Norman rule of England

cards.front: In which 1066 battle did William the Conqueror defeat King Harold II to take control of England?
cards.back: Battle of Hastings

cards.front: Battle of the Alamo (1836)
cards.back: Famous defeat during the Texas Revolution; Texan defenders held the Alamo mission in San Antonio against the Mexican army for 13 days

cards.front: Which 1836 battle became a rallying cry ("Remember the Alamo!") during the Texas Revolution?
cards.back: Battle of the Alamo

cards.front: Battle of San Jacinto (1836)
cards.back: Decisive battle of the Texas Revolution; Sam Houston's Texan forces defeated Santa Anna's Mexican army, winning Texas independence

cards.front: Which battle won Texas its independence from Mexico?
cards.back: Battle of San Jacinto (1836)

cards.front: Battle of Waterloo (1815)
cards.back: Final defeat of Napoleon Bonaparte; British and Prussian forces ended his rule in present-day Belgium

cards.front: In which battle was Napoleon Bonaparte finally defeated for good?
cards.back: Battle of Waterloo (1815)

cards.front: Battle of Little Bighorn (1876)
cards.back: Lakota, Northern Cheyenne, and Arapaho forces defeated U.S. Army troops under Lt. Col. George Custer; also called Custer's Last Stand

cards.front: What battle is also known as Custer's Last Stand?
cards.back: Battle of Little Bighorn (1876)
